Proposition 3 
                         
                            Let  
                            
                               μ 
                             
                            be a polytomous attribution. Then the following are equivalent. 
                         
                         
                            (1)  
                            
                               
                                  μ 
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            
                            . 
                         
                         
                            (2)  
                            
                               μ 
                             
                            is a derived polytomous attribution. 
                         
                         
                            (3)  
                            
                               μ 
                             
                            is complete.

Let  
                            
                               μ 
                             
                            be a polytomous attribution. Then the following are equivalent.

(1)  
                            
                               
                                  μ 
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            
                            .

(2)  
                            
                               μ 
                             
                            is a derived polytomous attribution.

(3)  
                            
                               μ 
                             
                            is complete.

Proof 
                         (1)  
                               ⟹ 
                             (2): Let  
                               
                                  μ 
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              K 
                                           
                                           
                                              μ 
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               
                            . Put  
                               
                                  K 
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            . Then  
                               
                                  μ 
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            . That is,  
                               μ 
                             is a derived polytomous attribution. 
                         (2)  
                               ⟹ 
                             (3): Let  
                               μ 
                             be a derived polytomous attribution. That is,  
                               
                                  μ 
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                  
                               
                             for some polytomous structure  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                            . Let  
                               
                                  q 
                                  ∈ 
                                  Q 
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  C 
                                  ∈ 
                                  μ 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                            . Then there is  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                                  ∖ 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     Φ 
                                     } 
                                  
                               
                             such that  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ⊑ 
                                  C 
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  K 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  C 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                            . Firstly,  
                               
                                  Ω 
                                  ∈ 
                                  
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  μ 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                             from  Proposition 2 (2). This shows that  
                               μ 
                             satisfies  Definition 5 (1). Secondly, if  
                               
                                  C 
                                  ⊑ 
                                  D 
                                  ∈ 
                                  
                                     
                                        L 
                                     
                                     
                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  C 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  D 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                            , then  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ⊑ 
                                  D 
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  K 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  D 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                            . It follows that  
                               
                                  D 
                                  ∈ 
                                  
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  μ 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                            . This shows that  
                               μ 
                             satisfies  Definition 5 (2). Thirdly,  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∈ 
                                  
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     r 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  μ 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     r 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                             for each  
                               
                                  r 
                                  ∈ 
                                  Q 
                               
                             from  Proposition 2 (2). It follows that  
                               μ 
                             satisfies  Definition 5 (3). Consequently,  
                               μ 
                             is complete. 
                         (3)  
                               ⟹ 
                             (1): Let  
                               μ 
                             be complete. By  Lemma 4 (1),  
                               
                                  μ 
                                  ≾ 
                                  
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            . It follows from  Lemma 2 (2) that  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                                  ⊆ 
                                  μ 
                               
                            . Let  
                               
                                  q 
                                  ∈ 
                                  Q 
                               
                            . It suffices to prove that  
                               
                                  μ 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  ⊆ 
                                  
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                            . Assume that  
                               
                                  C 
                                  ∈ 
                                  μ 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                            . By  Definition 5 (3), there is  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∈ 
                                  
                                     
                                        L 
                                     
                                     
                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                                  ∖ 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     Φ 
                                     } 
                                  
                               
                             such that  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ⊑ 
                                  C 
                               
                            ,  
                               
                                  K 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  C 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∈ 
                                  μ 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     r 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                             for all  
                               
                                  r 
                                  ∈ 
                                  Q 
                               
                            . So  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∈ 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∘ 
                                     
                                  
                               
                             from  Proposition 1 (1). Notice that  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                  
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∘ 
                                     
                                  
                                  ⋃ 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     Φ 
                                     , 
                                     Ω 
                                     } 
                                  
                               
                            . So  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∈ 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                  
                                  ∖ 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     Φ 
                                     } 
                                  
                               
                            . It follows that  
                               
                                  C 
                                  ∈ 
                                  
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              K 
                                           
                                           
                                              μ 
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                            . Thus,  
                               
                                  μ 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  ⊆ 
                                  
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                             is proved. □

                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                  
                                  ∖ 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     Φ 
                                     } 
                                  
                               
                            . It follows that  
                               
                                  C 
                                  ∈ 
                                  
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              K 
                                           
                                           
                                              μ 
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                            . Thus,  
                               
                                  μ 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  ⊆ 
                                  
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                             is proved. □

Remark 3 
                         For any polytomous attribution  
                               μ

                                     μ 
                                  
                                  
                                     ∗

                                        μ

                             is a derived polytomous attribution.

Proposition 4 
                         
                            Let  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            be a polytomous structure. Then the following are equivalent. 
                         
                         
                            (1)  
                            
                               
                                  K 
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            
                            . 
                         
                         
                            (2)  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            is a derived polytomous structure. 
                         
                         
                            (3)  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            is complete.

Let  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            be a polytomous structure. Then the following are equivalent.

(1)  
                            
                               
                                  K 
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            
                            .

(2)

                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            is a derived polytomous structure.

(3)  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            is complete.

Proof 
                         (1)  
                               ⟹ 
                             (2): Let  
                               
                                  K 
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              μ 
                                           
                                           
                                              K 
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               
                            . Put  
                               
                                  μ 
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            . Then  
                               
                                  K 
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            . That is,  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             is a derived polytomous structure. 
                         (2)  
                               ⟹ 
                             (3): Let  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             be a derived polytomous structure. That is, there is a polytomous attribution  
                               μ 
                             such that  
                               
                                  K 
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        μ

                                  μ 
                                  ≾ 
                                  
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            . It follows that

                                              ∗ 
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                  ⊆ 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                  
                               
                             from  Lemma 3 (1). On the other hand,  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                  
                                  ⊆ 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                               
                             from  Lemma 4 (2). Note that  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        K

                                              μ 
                                           
                                           
                                              ∗

                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              μ 
                                           
                                           
                                              ∗

                                  K 
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        K

                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              μ 
                                           
                                           
                                              ∗ 
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               
                            . Since  
                               
                                  
                                     μ 
                                  
                                  
                                     ∗ 
                                  
                               
                             is complete from  Remark 3 ,

                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  L

                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             is derived from the complete polytomous attribution  
                               
                                  
                                     μ 
                                  
                                  
                                     ∗ 
                                  
                               
                            . Thusly,  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             is complete. 
                         (3)  
                               ⟹ 
                             (1): Let

(1)

Corollary 3

                               ⊆ 
                            
                            ) complete polytomous structure containing  
                            
                               K 
                            
                            .

                               K

Proof

In order to avoid assuming granularity in the Galois connection that was established in Theorem 8.5.3 of  Falmagne and Doignon (2011) ,  Shen et al. (2021)  used the derivations between knowledge structures and attributions to establish a Galois connection between the collection of all knowledge structures on the domain  
                         Q 
                       and the collection of all attributions on  
                         Q 
                      , where the closed elements in the two collections are the knowledge spaces and complete attributions respectively (see Theorem 2 of  Shen et al., 2021 ). As an interesting generalization of this result in the polytomous case, the following theorem establishes a Galois connection between polytomous structures and polytomous attributions. Beforehand, we give some notations for convenience.

Notation 3 
                         (1)  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  K 
                                  , 
                                  ⊆ 
                                  ) 
                               
                             denotes a partially ordered set, where  
                               K 
                             is the collection of all polytomous structures and  
                               ⊆ 
                             is the relation of set inclusion. 
                         (2)  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  F 
                                  , 
                                  ≾ 
                                  ) 
                               
                             denotes a quasi ordered set described as in  Notation 2 (2).

(1)  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  K 
                                  , 
                                  ⊆ 
                                  ) 
                               
                             denotes a partially ordered set, where  
                               K 
                             is the collection of all polytomous structures and  
                               ⊆ 
                             is the relation of set inclusion.

(2)  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  F 
                                  , 
                                  ≾ 
                                  ) 
                               
                             denotes a quasi ordered set described as in  Notation 2 (2).

Theorem 1 
                         
                            Define two mappings  
                            
                               
                                  f 
                                  : 
                                  K 
                                  ⟶ 
                                  F 
                               
                             
                            and  
                            
                               
                                  h 
                                  : 
                                  F 
                                  ⟶ 
                                  K 
                               
                             
                            by  
                            
                               
                                  f 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     K 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                  
                               
                             
                            and  
                            
                               
                                  h 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     μ 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                  
                               
                             
                            for  
                            
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                               
                             
                            and  
                            
                               
                                  μ 
                                  ∈ 
                                  F 
                               
                            
                            , respectively. Then the pair  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  f 
                                  , 
                                  h 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            of mappings is a Galois connection between  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  K 
                                  , 
                                  ⊆ 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            and  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  F 
                                  , 
                                  ≾ 
                                  ) 
                               
                            
                            . Further, the closed elements of this Galois connection  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  f 
                                  , 
                                  h 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            in  
                            
                               K 
                             
                            are the complete polytomous structures, and in  
                            
                               F 
                             
                            the complete polytomous attributions. Moreover, this Galois connection  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  f 
                                  , 
                                  h 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            induces between the two sets of closed elements the one-to-one correspondence

Remark 5 
                         (1) In Theorem 1, the closed elements of  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  f 
                                  , 
                                  h 
                                  ) 
                               
                             in  
                               K 
                             and  
                               F 
                             are  
                               
                                  h 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     F 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  f 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     K 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                             respectively, which is similar to  Lemma 1 (iii) of  Heller (2021) . 
                         (2) Let  
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                                  
                                     s 
                                  
                               
                             be the collection of all polytomous spaces and  
                               
                                  
                                     f 
                                  
                                  
                                     s 
                                  
                               
                             be the restriction of  
                               f 
                             on  
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                                  
                                     s 
                                  
                               
                            , where  
                               f 
                             is described as in  Theorem 1 . Then  
                               
                                  
                                     f 
                                  
                                  
                                     s 
                                  
                               
                             is a one-to-one correspondence between  
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                                  
                                     s 
                                  
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        f 
                                     
                                     
                                        s 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           s 
                                        
                                     
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                            . Here,  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        f 
                                     
                                     
                                        s 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           s 
                                        
                                     
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                             is exactly the collection of all polytomous attributions derived from polytomous spaces. However, we have not a direct characterization of elements in  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        f 
                                     
                                     
                                        s 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           s 
                                        
                                     
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                            . That is, we do not know how to characterize the polytomous attributions derived from polytomous spaces.

(1) In Theorem 1, the closed elements of

In the dichotomous case, it is known that (1) and (2) in  Lemma 5  are equivalent (i.e., in KST, the derived knowledge structures and knowledge spaces are equivalent). However, (2) and (3) in  Lemma 5  need not be equivalent even in the dichotomous case. Naturally, an interesting question arises: under what assumption would (1) and (2) in  Lemma 5  be equivalent? In the following, we give an assumption under which (1) and (2) in  Lemma 5  are equivalent.

Assumption 1 
                         Assume that  
                               
                                  L 
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        L 
                                     
                                     
                                        + 
                                     
                                  
                                  ∪ 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     0 
                                     } 
                                  
                               
                             for the complete lattice  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  L 
                                  , 
                                  ≤ 
                                  ) 
                               
                            .

Assume that  
                               
                                  L 
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        L 
                                     
                                     
                                        + 
                                     
                                  
                                  ∪ 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     0 
                                     } 
                                  
                               
                             for the complete lattice  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  L 
                                  , 
                                  ≤ 
                                  ) 
                               
                            .

There exist many complete lattices satisfying  Assumption 1 . For example, the complete lattice  
                         
                            ( 
                            L 
                            , 
                            ≤ 
                            ) 
                         
                       in  Example 4  of  Stefanutti et al. (2020)  satisfies  Assumption 1 . In addition, it is known that a finite linearly ordered set  
                         
                            ( 
                            L 
                            , 
                            ≤ 
                            ) 
                         
                       satisfies  Assumption 1  (like, e.g., many rating scales, see  Stefanutti et al., 2020 ).

Lemma 6 
                         
                            Let  
                            
                               μ 
                             
                            be a polytomous attribution. If  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  L 
                                  , 
                                  ≤ 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            satisfies

                            is a polytomous space.

Corollary 4 
                         
                            Let  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            be a polytomous structure. If

                                  , 
                                  ≤ 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            satisfies  
                            
                               Assumption  1 
                            , Then the following are equivalent.

                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            is a polytomous space. 
                         
                         
                            (2)  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            is a derived polytomous structure.

Definition 7 
                         
                            Stefanutti et al., 2020 
                         
                         Let  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             be a polytomous structure. For  
                               
                                  l 
                                  ∈ 
                                  L 
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  q 
                                  ∈ 
                                  Q 
                               
                            , a state  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                               
                             is an  
                               l 
                            -atom at  
                               q 
                             if the following three conditions are all satisfied: 
                         (1)  
                               
                                  l 
                                  ∈ 
                                  
                                     
                                        L 
                                     
                                     
                                        + 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            ; 
                         (2)  
                               
                                  K 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  l 
                               
                            ; 
                         (3)  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ′ 
                                     
                                  
                                  ⊑ 
                                  K 
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ′ 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  l 
                               
                             imply  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ′ 
                                     
                                  
                                  = 
                                  K 
                               
                             for all  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ′ 
                                     
                                  
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                               
                            . 
                         Then  
                               K 
                             is said to be an atom at  
                               q 
                             if it is an  
                               l 
                            -atom at  
                               q 
                             for some  
                               
                                  l 
                                  ∈ 
                                  
                                     
                                        L 
                                     
                                     
                                        + 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            . Moreover, it is called an  
                               l 
                            -atom if it is an  
                               l 
                            -atom at some  
                               
                                  q 
                                  ∈ 
                                  Q 
                               
                            .

Remark 7 
                         Let

                                     {

                                  1 
                                  ∈ 
                                  
                                     
                                        L

                            , i.e.,  
                               Ω

                                  = 
                                  Ω 
                               
                            . This shows that  
                               Ω

                               Ω

                               1 
                            -atom at  
                               q 
                            .

Definition 8 
                         
                            Stefanutti et al., 2020 
                         
                         A polytomous structure  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             is said to be granular if for any state  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                               
                            , any item  
                               
                                  q 
                                  ∈ 
                                  Q 
                               
                            , and any response value  
                               
                                  l 
                                  ∈ 
                                  L 
                                  ∖ 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     0 
                                     } 
                                  
                               
                            ,  
                               
                                  K 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  l 
                               
                             implies that there is an  
                               l 
                            -atom at  
                               q 
                             that is dominated by  
                               K 
                            .

A polytomous structure  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             is said to be granular if for any state  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                               
                            , any item  
                               
                                  q 
                                  ∈ 
                                  Q 
                               
                            , and any response value  
                               
                                  l 
                                  ∈ 
                                  L 
                                  ∖ 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     0 
                                     } 
                                  
                               
                            ,  
                               
                                  K 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  l 
                               
                             implies that there is an  
                               l 
                            -atom at  
                               q 
                             that is dominated by  
                               K 
                            .

In KST, any finite knowledge space is granular and any granular knowledge space has a base (see Definition 3.6.1 and Theorem 3.6.6 of  Falmagne & Doignon, 2011 , respectively). Then, are there analogous results in the polytomous case? Recently,  Stefanutti et al. (2020)  gave an example to show that a finite polytomous space need not be granular (see Example 3 of  Stefanutti et al., 2020 ). However, does any finite polytomous space (structure) have a base? Also, does any granular polytomous space (structure) have a base? These questions are still open and interesting.

Remark 9

                               μ

                                  0̸

Proposition 6 
                         
                            Let  
                            
                               μ 
                             
                            be a polytomous attribution. If  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     μ 
                                  
                                  
                                     ∗

                               ⋇

                                        ̂

                                                 ∗

                                  L 
                                  , 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                  
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            is a granular polytomous structure.

                               μ

Corollary 5 
                         
                            Let  
                            
                               μ 
                             
                            be a polytomous attribution satisfying Condition ( 
                            
                               ⋇ 
                            
                            ). If

                            is a granular polytomous structure.

Similar to the granularity of the attribution in ordinary (dichotomous) KST (see Definition 8.5.2 of  Falmagne & Doignon, 2011 ), we introduce the granularity of the polytomous attribution.

Definition 9 
                         A polytomous attribution  
                               μ 
                             is called granular if the polytomous structure  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  , 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                  
                                  ) 
                               
                             is granular.

A polytomous attribution  
                               μ 
                             is called granular if the polytomous structure  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  , 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                  
                                  ) 
                               
                             is granular.

Definition 9 gives the concept of a granular polytomous attribution by the property that such a polytomous attribution derives a granular polytomous structure. The following proposition gives a direct characterization of granular polytomous attributions. Such a characterization would be useful in Galois connection between the collection of all granular polytomous structures and the collection of all polytomous granular attributions.

Proposition 7 
                         
                            Let  
                            
                               μ 
                             
                            be a polytomous attribution. Then the following are equivalent. 
                         
                         
                            (1)  
                            
                               μ 
                             
                            is a granular. 
                         
                         
                            (2)  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 ( 
                                                 
                                                    
                                                       μ 
                                                    
                                                    
                                                       ∗ 
                                                    
                                                 
                                                 ) 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              + 
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        ̂ 
                                     
                                  
                                  ≈ 
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           
                                              
                                                 μ 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ∗ 
                                              
                                           
                                           ) 
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        +

                                     μ 
                                  
                                  
                                     ∗ 
                                  
                               
                             
                            satisfies Condition ( 
                            
                               ⋇ 
                            
                            ).
