As a direct result of  Propositions 5 ,  7  and  Definition 9 , we have the following proposition.

Proposition 8 
                         
                            (1) If  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            is a granular polytomous structure, then  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     μ 
                                  
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                               
                             
                            is a granular polytomous attribution. 
                         
                         
                            (2) If  
                            
                               μ 
                             
                            is a granular polytomous attribution, then  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  , 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                  
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            is a granular polytomous structure.

(1) If  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  , 
                                  K

                            is a granular polytomous structure, then  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     μ 
                                  
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                               
                             
                            is a granular polytomous attribution.

(2) If  
                            
                               μ 
                             
                            is a granular polytomous attribution, then  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  , 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                  
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            is a granular polytomous structure.

In KST, by adding granularity in knowledge structures and attributions,  Falmagne and Doignon (2011)  established a Galois connection between the collection of all granular knowledge structures on the arbitrary domain  
                         Q 
                       and the collection of all granular attributions on  
                         Q 
                      , where the closed elements in the two collections are the granular knowledge spaces and the surmise functions respectively (see Theorem 8.5.3 of  Falmagne & Doignon, 2011 ). As an interesting generalization of this result to the polytomous case, the following theorem establishes a Galois connection between granular polytomous structures and granular polytomous attributions.

Theorem 2 
                         
                            Let  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                                  
                                     g 
                                  
                               
                             
                            be the collection of all granular polytomous structures, and  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     F 
                                  
                                  
                                     g 
                                  
                               
                             
                            the collection of all granular polytomous attributions. Additionally, two mappings  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        f 
                                     
                                     
                                        g 
                                     
                                  
                                  : 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        g 
                                     
                                  
                                  ⟶ 
                                  
                                     
                                        F 
                                     
                                     
                                        g 
                                     
                                  
                               
                             
                            and  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        h 
                                     
                                     
                                        g 
                                     
                                  
                                  : 
                                  
                                     
                                        F 
                                     
                                     
                                        g 
                                     
                                  
                                  ⟶ 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        g 
                                     
                                  
                               
                             
                            are defined by  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        f 
                                     
                                     
                                        g 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     K 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                  
                               
                             
                            and  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        h 
                                     
                                     
                                        g 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     μ 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        μ 
                                     
                                  
                               
                             
                            for  
                            
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∈ 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        g 
                                     
                                  
                               
                             
                            and  
                            
                               
                                  μ 
                                  ∈ 
                                  
                                     
                                        F 
                                     
                                     
                                        g 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            
                            , respectively. That is,  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     f 
                                  
                                  
                                     g 
                                  
                               
                             
                            and  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     h 
                                  
                                  
                                     g 
                                  
                               
                             
                            are the restrictions of  
                            
                               f 
                             
                            and  
                            
                               h 
                             
                            on  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                                  
                                     g 
                                  
                               
                             
                            and  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     F 
                                  
                                  
                                     g 
                                  
                               
                             
                            respectively, where  
                            
                               f 
                             
                            and  
                            
                               h 
                             
                            are described as in  
                            
                               Theorem  1 
                            . Then the following hold. 
                         
                         
                            (1) The pair  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  
                                     
                                        f 
                                     
                                     
                                        g 
                                     
                                  
                                  , 
                                  
                                     
                                        h 
                                     
                                     
                                        g 
                                     
                                  
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            of mappings is a Galois connection between  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        g 
                                     
                                  
                                  , 
                                  ⊆ 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            and  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  
                                     
                                        F 
                                     
                                     
                                        g 
                                     
                                  
                                  , 
                                  ≾ 
                                  ) 
                               
                            
                            . 
                         
                         
                            (2) The closed elements of  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  
                                     
                                        f 
                                     
                                     
                                        g 
                                     
                                  
                                  , 
                                  
                                     
                                        h 
                                     
                                     
                                        g 
                                     
                                  
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            in  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                                  
                                     g 
                                  
                               
                             
                            are the complete granular polytomous structures, and in  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     F

Let

                                        g 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            
                            , respectively. That is,

                                     g

                               f

(1) The pair

                                        h

                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            of mappings is a Galois connection between

The proof of  Theorem 2  is Similar to that of  Theorem 1 , which can be completed by drawing upon  Lemmas 3 ,  4  and  Propositions 3 ,  4 ,  8 . We omit the proof.

Remark 10 
                         (1) In  Theorem 2 , the closed elements of  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  
                                     
                                        f 
                                     
                                     
                                        g 
                                     
                                  
                                  , 
                                  
                                     
                                        h 
                                     
                                     
                                        g 
                                     
                                  
                                  ) 
                               
                             in  
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                                  
                                     g 
                                  
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  
                                     F 
                                  
                                  
                                     g 
                                  
                               
                             are  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        h 
                                     
                                     
                                        g 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     
                                        
                                           F 
                                        
                                        
                                           g 
                                        
                                     
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        f 
                                     
                                     
                                        g 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           g 
                                        
                                     
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                             respectively, which is similar to  Lemma 1 (iii) of  Heller (2021) . Here,  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        f 
                                     
                                     
                                        g 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           g 
                                        
                                     
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                             is exactly the collection of all polytomous attributions derived from granular polytomous structures. Thus, we have a direct characterization of the polytomous attributions in  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        f 
                                     
                                     
                                        g 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           g 
                                        
                                     
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                            : a polytomous attribution is derived from a granular polytomous structure if and only if it is a complete granular polytomous attribution. 
                         (2) Let  
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                                  
                                     ⊔ 
                                  
                               
                             be the collection of all granular polytomous spaces (see Theorem 11 of  Stefanutti et al., 2020 ) and  
                               
                                  
                                     f 
                                  
                                  
                                     ⊔ 
                                  
                               
                             be the restriction of  
                               f 
                             on  
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                                  
                                     ⊔ 
                                  
                               
                             where  
                               f 
                             is described as in  Theorem 1 . Then  
                               
                                  
                                     f 
                                  
                                  
                                     ⊔ 
                                  
                               
                             is a one-to-one correspondence between  
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                                  
                                     ⊔ 
                                  
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        f 
                                     
                                     
                                        ⊔ 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           ⊔ 
                                        
                                     
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                            . Here,  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        f 
                                     
                                     
                                        ⊔ 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           ⊔ 
                                        
                                     
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                             is exactly the collection of all polytomous attributions derived from granular polytomous spaces. However, we do not know how to characterize polytomous attributions in  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        f 
                                     
                                     
                                        ⊔ 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           ⊔ 
                                        
                                     
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                            . 
                         (3) Let  
                               
                                  
                                     M 
                                  
                                  
                                     ⊔

In this paper, we give two derivations that show some relations between polytomous structures and polytomous attributions.

(1) Let  
                         μ 
                       be a polytomous attribution. Put  
                         
                            
                               
                                  K

With the help of the two derivations, we establish two Galois connections around (granular) polytomous structures and (granular) polytomous attributions. More precisely, let  
                         Q 
                       be a nonempty set of items and  
                         
                            ( 
                            L 
                            , 
                            ≤ 
                            ) 
                         
                       a complete lattice consisting of a nonempty set  
                         L 
                       of response values with a partial order  
                         ≤ 
                       on  
                         L 
                      . All polytomous structures and all polytomous attribution are on the pair  
                         
                            ( 
                            Q 
                            , 
                            L 
                            ) 
                         
                      . The derived polytomous structure and the derived polytomous attribution are the polytomous structure and the polytomous attribution derived from some polytomous attribution and some polytomous structure, respectively. Granular polytomous structures and granular polytomous attributions are described as in  Definitions 8 and 9 , respectively. Additionally, let  
                         ⊆ 
                       be the relation of set inclusion and  
                         ≾ 
                       the quasi order described as in  Notation 2 (2).  
                         
                            
                               L 
                            
                            
                               + 
                            
                         
                       denotes the subset of all the irreducible response values of  
                         L 
                       where a single response value  
                         
                            x 
                            ∈ 
                            L 
                         
                       is irreducible if, for all subsets  
                         
                            G 
                            ⊆ 
                            L 
                         
                      ,  
                         
                            sup 
                            G 
                            = 
                            x 
                         
                       implies  
                         
                            x 
                            ∈ 
                            G 
                         
                      .

                            f

                               K

                            = 
                            
                               
                                  μ

                            ∈

                       for every

                       are described as in the above (1) and (2) respectively. Further, the closed elements of

                            f

                         K 
                       are the complete polytomous structures, and in

                       induces a one-to-one correspondence between the two sets of closed elements. Specially, if every element in
